issues: outline_color_mismatch: thick green/teal chunky border vs thin dark 1px outlines on all peers
outline_weight_mismatch: candidate border is 3-4px dithered pattern vs hairline peer outlines
highlight_intensity_extreme: blown-out white core occupies majority of sprite vs tightly controlled specular dots on peers
rendering_style_divergence: flat glow/UI-heart treatment vs classic pixel character art with shading passes on peers
shadow_absent: no directional drop-shadow present; peers consistently show 1 shadow
detail_density_low: simple 2-tone heart form vs dense multi-element character sprites
palette_overlap_weak: white + green + isolated pink dots share almost no hues with warm/saturated peer palette
charm_level_mismatch: execution reads clinical UI icon rather than the endearing creature personality peers exhibit
reasoning: LEFT panel shows ~50 Denzi character/creature sprites: consistent thin dark outlines, rich warm-to-neutral saturated palettes, controlled highlights, single directional shadow, moderate-to-high detail density, and strong creature 'charm.' RIGHT panel candidate vfx_heal_3_v07 is a large heart silhouette dominated by a blown-white interior and a thick dithered green border. At least 6 of the 11 dimensions fail hard: outline color (green vs black), outline weight (chunky vs hairline), highlight intensity (overexposed core vs pinpoint spec), rendering style (luminous glow vs opaque pixel character), shadow (absent vs present), and charm level (UI icon vs cute creature). Detail density and palette overlap are also significantly below peer baseline. The only partial alignments are the pixel grid structure and a few stray pink sparkle dots that loosely echo peer accent colors. This candidate does not belong in the same sprite family without a substantial rework of its border treatment, value range, and shadow pass.
